title: Lydia Jin
description: Lydia Jin has served as Senior Director of Strategic Marketing and Communications at Duke Kunshan University since October 2016. text:
Lydia Jin
Lydia Jin has served as Senior Director of Strategic Marketing and Communications at Duke Kunshan University since October 2016. She leads the university’s communications and branding initiatives in China and globally.
Lydia began her career at Xinhua News Agency’s International News Department, where her work on international relations was published by Xinhua and many Chinese and English media outlets. In recognition of her contributions, she received the Young Professional Award from Xinhua News Agency in 1996.
Before joining Duke Kunshan University, Lydia was Director of Communications and External Affairs at Otis Elevator (China) Investment Co., Ltd., overseeing corporate communications and government affairs. In 2013, she led her team to win the Otis Global President Award, marking the first time in the company’s 160-year history that a communications team received this honor. Previously, she was a brand manager at Beijing Benz-DaimlerChrysler Automobile Co., Ltd., where she managed communications for both the Chrysler and Mercedes-Benz brands.
A World Bank Scholar, Lydia earned a master’s degree in International Development Policy from Duke University’s Sanford School of Public Policy. She also holds a BA in British and American Literature from Beijing Foreign Studies University.
Beyond her leadership roles, Lydia is also a passionate writer and a member of the Kunshan Writers’ Association. She has contributed to several publications, including editing the English edition of China Almanac during her time at Xinhua. While at Otis, she collaborated with the General Administration of Quality Supervision, Inspection and Quarantine to launch and co-edit a popular science book. The book is available in over 100 libraries across China, including the National Library of China, and later received awards from the Ministry of Science and Technology and the State Administration of Radio, Film, Television, and Publishing.
